meta.question: What external systems interface with the APS at the telescope level?

events.output: I'll explore the APS (Alignment and Phasing System) element to understand its structure and interfaces at the telescope level. Let me start by finding the APS element.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("aps")))
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found many APS-related elements. I need to find the APS block at the telescope level and explore its interfaces/connectors to identify external systems. Let me look for the APS block specifically and its ports/connectors.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), "aps") && 
    ?type IN (vocab:block, vocab:system%20component, vocab:component)
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found several APS blocks. The most relevant for telescope-level interfaces are "APS Black Box Specification TMT" (under Project Systems Engineering) and "APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL". I also see interface definitions like "APS-M1CS". Let me explore the APS Black Box Specification TMT block's properties and connectors to find external system interfaces.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name "APS Black Box Specification TMT" .
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
}
GROUP BY ?prop
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Exact name match failed, likely due to URL encoding. Let me use CONTAINS filter instead to find the properties of the APS Black Box Specification TMT block.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), "aps black box specification tmt"))
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
}
GROUP BY ?prop
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The name might be URL-encoded in the data. Let me try using the URI directly from the earlier results, or try a different approach - look for the name with %20 encoding.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(STR(?name) = "APS%20Black%20Box%20Specification%20TMT")
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
}
GROUP BY ?prop
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found properties for APS Black Box Specification TMT. It has directed relationships (source/target), typed elements, and owned elements. Let me now look at the connectors and ports of this block, and also explore the interface definitions at the telescope level. Let me also check the APS Operational Domain block which might show external systems.

The directed relationship query returned empty. Let me try a different approach - look at the APS Operational Domain block which likely shows external systems that interact with APS. Also, let me look for interface definitions that mention APS at the telescope level.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?name ?type WHERE {
  ?pkg vocab:NamedElement_name ?pname .
  FILTER(STR(?pname) = "APS%20Operational%20Domain%20to-be")
  ?pkg vocab:Element_ownedElement ?child .
  ?child vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  ?child rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Let me try with URL-encoded name. Also, let me try a broader approach - look for interface definitions at the telescope level that mention APS, and look for part properties within the APS Operational Domain block.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?name ?type WHERE {
  ?pkg vocab:NamedElement_name ?pname .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?pname), "APS%20Operational%20Domain"))
  ?pkg vocab:Element_ownedElement ?child .
  ?child vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  ?child rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The ownedElement approach isn't working. Let me try using StructuredClassifier_ownedAttribute or part properties to find what's inside the APS domain block. Also, let me look at the Interface Definitions folder at the telescope level for APS interfaces.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?ifName ?ifType WHERE {
  ?iface vocab:NamedElement_name ?ifName .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?ifName), "APS"))
  ?iface rdf:type ?ifType .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(STR(?iface), "Interface%2520Definitions") ||
    CONTAINS(STR(?iface), "Interface%20Definitions")
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found APS-M1CS interface definitions. Now I need to find ALL interface definitions involving APS, not just M1CS. Let me search more broadly for all interface packages/blocks at the telescope level that involve APS.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?ifName ?ifType WHERE {
  ?iface vocab:NamedElement_name ?ifName .
  ?iface rdf:type ?ifType .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(STR(?iface), "Interface%2520Definitions") &&
    ?ifType = vocab:package
  )
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?ifName), "APS"))
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Only found APS-M1CS as an interface definition package. But there might be more interfaces defined differently. Let me look at the APS block's ports and connectors at the telescope level, and also check for connectors involving APS in the telescope structure.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?portName ?portType WHERE {
  ?aps vocab:NamedElement_name ?apsName .
  FILTER(STR(?apsName) = "APS%20Black%20Box%20Specification%20TMT")
  ?aps vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
  ?port vocab:NamedElement_name ?portName .
  ?port rdf:type ?portType .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: No ports on the TMT-level APS block. Let me try the APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL block which is the APS work package level block, and also look for connectors that connect to APS part properties within a parent block (like the telescope structure).

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?partName ?partType ?ownerName WHERE {
  ?part vocab:NamedElement_name ?partName .
  ?part rdf:type vocab:part%20property .
  ?part vocab:TypedElement_type ?apsType .
  ?apsType vocab:NamedElement_name ?apsTypeName .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?apsTypeName), "APS"))
  ?part vocab:Element_owner ?owner .
  ?owner vocab:NamedElement_name ?ownerName .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Let me try a different approach. Let me look for part properties whose name contains APS and see what parent blocks they belong to, and then find sibling parts (which would be the other systems at the same level).

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?partName ?ownerName WHERE {
  ?part vocab:NamedElement_name ?partName .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?partName), "APS") || CONTAINS(STR(?partName), "aps") || CONTAINS(STR(?partName), "Alignment%20and%20Phasing"))
  ?part rdf:type vocab:part%20property .
  ?part vocab:Element_owner ?owner .
  ?owner vocab:NamedElement_name ?ownerName .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The name encoding is causing issues. Let me try with the URL-encoded %20 in the FILTER for the name field, since the names seem to be stored with %20 encoding.

Found many APS part properties. The owner names are empty which is odd. I see "aPS" as a part property - this is likely the APS part within a telescope-level block. Let me find the owner of the "aPS" part property and then find sibling parts and connectors to identify external systems.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?part rdf:type vocab:part%20property .
  ?part vocab:NamedElement_name ?partName .
  FILTER(STR(?partName) = "aPS")
  ?part ?prop ?value .
}
GROUP BY ?prop
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found properties for the "aPS" part property. It has Element_owner, TypedElement_type, and ConnectableElement__structuredClassifierOfRole. Let me get the owner (parent block), the type, and then find connectors that connect to this part.
Owner name retrieval failed. Let me get the raw values of owner and type for the aPS part property to see what they look like.

The "aPS" part is inside a view for "Acquisition & Guiding" use case. This is a view-level element, not the structural telescope-level interface. Let me take a different approach and look for connectors that connect APS ports to other system ports at the telescope level. Let me search for connectors within the telescope structure that involve APS.
Let me try exploring the properties of the APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL block to see what properties it has, and also try looking for ports on any APS block.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>

SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(STR(?name) = "APS%20Operational%20Blackbox%20Specification%20JPL")
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
}
GROUP BY ?prop
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL block has 13 owned ports, 21 part properties, and connectors. This is the detailed APS block. Let me get its ports and then find what external systems connect to those ports via connectors.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?portName ?portType WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(STR(?name) = "APS%20Operational%20Blackbox%20Specification%20JPL")
  ?elem vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
  ?port vocab:NamedElement_name ?portName .
  OPTIONAL { ?port rdf:type ?portType }
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The name matching is failing again. Let me use the URI directly from the earlier results to query the ports.
URI approach also failed. The URI encoding might differ. Let me try using CONTAINS on the URI string to match the element, then get its ports.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?portName ?portType WHERE {
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?ename .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?ename), "APS%20Operational%20Blackbox%20Specification%20JPL"))
  ?elem vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
  ?port vocab:NamedElement_name ?portName .
  OPTIONAL { ?port rdf:type ?portType }
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Let me try getting the raw port URIs without requiring a name, to see if the ports exist but have different name encoding.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?port WHERE {
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS%2520Operational%2520Blackbox%2520Specification%2520JPL") || CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS%20Operational%20Blackbox%20Specification%20JPL"))
  ?elem vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Excellent! Found 13 ports on the APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL block. The port names reveal the external systems that interface with APS: TCS (PEAS2TCSIn/Out), M1CS (PEAS2M1CSIn/Out), ESW (PEAS2ESWIn/Out, GC2ESWIn/Out), CS (PEAS2CSIn/Out), and User (APS2UserIn/Out). Also GCDataOut. Let me now also check the APS Black Box Specification TMT block for telescope-level interfaces, and verify these port names properly.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?port WHERE {
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS%2520Black%2520Box%2520Specification%2520TMT"))
  ?elem vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The TMT-level APS block has no ports. The JPL block is the one with the detailed interfaces. Let me now also look for connectors that connect to these APS ports to confirm the external systems, and also check if there are any dependency relationships (like interface realizations) involving APS.

Got one empty row - the connector approach isn't yielding names. Let me try a proper connector query using the Connector_end predicate and ConnectorEnd_role, and also try to get the port names directly from the URIs I already have. The port names from the URIs are clear: PEAS2TCSIn, PEAS2TCSOut, PEAS2M1CSIn, PEAS2M1CSOut, PEAS2ESWIn, PEAS2ESWOut, PEAS2CSIn, PEAS2CSOut, GC2ESWIn, GC2ESWOut, GCDataOut, APS2UserIn, APS2UserOut.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?portName ?portTypeLabel WHERE {
  ?aps rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?aps), "APS%2520Operational%2520Blackbox%2520Specification%2520JPL"))
  ?aps vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
  BIND(REPLACE(STR(?port), "^.*/", "") AS ?portName)
  OPTIONAL {
    ?port vocab:TypedElement_type ?ptype .
    ?ptype vocab:NamedElement_name ?portTypeLabel .
  }
}
ORDER BY ?portName
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Now I have all 13 ports clearly listed. The port names reveal the external systems: TCS (Telescope Control System), M1CS (M1 Control System), ESW (Executive Software), CS (likely Communications System or Common Services), and User. Let me also check the APS Operational Domain block for additional context about external systems, and verify what CS stands for.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?partName WHERE {
  ?aps rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?aps), "APS%2520Operational%2520Blackbox%2520Specification%2520JPL"))
  ?aps vocab:StructuredClassifier_part ?part .
  BIND(REPLACE(STR(?part), "^.*/", "") AS ?partName)
}
ORDER BY ?partName
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Good, I can see the parts include the ports plus internal value properties. Now let me also check the APS Operational Domain block to see what external actors/systems are modeled there, and verify what CS stands for in the TMT context.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?portTypeName WHERE {
  ?aps rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?aps), "APS%2520Operational%2520Blackbox%2520Specification%2520JPL"))
  ?aps vocab:EncapsulatedClassifier_ownedPort ?port .
  ?port vocab:TypedElement_type ?ptype .
  ?ptype vocab:NamedElement_name ?portTypeName .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: No typed port info. Let me try to find what CS stands for by searching for blocks/systems with names starting with CS in the telescope context.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name ?type WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
  FILTER(
    ?type IN (vocab:block, vocab:system%20component, vocab:component) &&
    CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "Telescope") &&
    (
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Communications") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Common%20Services") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "CSW") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "GC") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Global%20Control") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "PEAS")
    )
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found PEAS references but not the full names for CS or GC. PEAS likely stands for Primary Edge/Alignment Sensor. Let me search more broadly for what CS and GC stand for in the TMT model.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS") && CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "Operational"))
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
  FILTER(?type IN (vocab:block, vocab:part%20property, vocab:port, vocab:proxy%20port))
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found "Procedure Executive and Analysis Software" which is likely what PEAS stands for. Also found "APS User" as an external actor. Let me now look at the APS Operational Domain block to find all external systems/actors that interact with APS.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?partName ?partType WHERE {
  ?domain rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?domain), "APS%2520Operational%2520Domain"))
  ?domain vocab:StructuredClassifier_part ?part .
  BIND(REPLACE(STR(?part), "^.*/", "") AS ?partName)
  ?part rdf:type ?partType .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Let me try to get the owned elements of the APS Operational Domain block using the URI pattern.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?child ?childType WHERE {
  ?domain rdf:type vocab:block .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?domain), "APS%2520Operational%2520Domain"))
  ?domain vocab:Element_ownedElement ?child .
  ?child rdf:type ?childType .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The APS Operational Domain block only has one owned element (an instance specification). The parts are likely inherited or referenced differently. Let me now focus on the port names I already have and try to identify what GC and CS stand for in the TMT context. Let me search for blocks with those abbreviations.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS") && 
    (
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "GC") || 
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Global") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Guider") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Guide%20Camera")
    )
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found "Global Configuration" which is likely what GC stands for. GC2ESW ports are for Global Configuration to Executive Software communication. Let me also check what CS stands for - it could be "Common Services" or "Communications Software".

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS") && 
    (
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "CS") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Common%20S") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Communication") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "Control%20S")
    )
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found "Common Services" and "PEAS-TCS", "PEAS-M3CS" which confirms CS likely stands for Common Services. Also found TCS and M1CS references. I also see "Communication, Software and Control Interfaces" as a category. Let me now do a final comprehensive query to get all the interface information clearly.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS") && 
    (
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "PEAS-M3CS") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "M3CS") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "M3") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "PCS") ||
      CONTAINS(STR(?name), "PEAS-PCS")
    )
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found PEAS-M3CS which confirms M3CS (M3 Control System) is another external system. Let me now also check for any other external systems by looking at the APS interface blocks and dependencies more broadly.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(STR(?elem), "APS") && 
    CONTAINS(STR(?name), "PEAS-")
  )
}
LIMIT 25
